input.task:
Build a workflow with:
1. A Manual Trigger node
2. A Wait node set to 1 second
3. A Set node named "running state test"

Connect them in order: Manual Trigger → Wait → "running state test" (Set node). No credentials needed.
input.conversationContext: User wants a simple 3-node workflow: Manual Trigger → Wait (1 second) → Set node named "running state test". No credentials involved. After build, the workflow will be run.

output.result: Error: direct builder calls require `bypassPlan: true` + an existing `workflowId` + a one-sentence `reason`. Use that combination for any edit to an existing workflow. For new workflows, multi-workflow builds, or data-table schema changes, call `plan` with a `build-workflow` task instead — the planner discovers credentials, data tables, and best practices, and schedules an orchestrator-run verification checkpoint.

input.summary: Build a minimal manual-trigger workflow with a 1-second Wait node followed by a Set node named 'running state test', then execute it.
assumptions: Wait node set to 1 second resume interval
Set node named exactly 'running state test' with no field modifications required

item.name: Running State Test
item.purpose: A simple test workflow triggered manually. It pauses for 1 second using a Wait node, then passes through a Set node named 'running state test'. The workflow exists to verify execution flow and running state behavior.

output.result: Added: Build 'Running State Test' workflow (1 item total)

item.title: Verify 'Running State Test' workflow runs successfully
item.instructions: Use the build outcome work item ID for wf-running-state-test to call verify-built-workflow. Confirm the execution completes without errors and passes through all three nodes: Manual Trigger → Wait (1 second) → Set ('running state test'). Pass condition: execution finishes with no errors and all nodes produce output.

output.result: Added: Verify 'Running State Test' workflow runs successfully (2 items total)

definitions.error: Node 'n8n-nodes-base.set' requires mode discriminator. Available modes: manual, raw.

input.name: Running State Test

input.result: Execution completed successfully in ~1 second. All three nodes (Manual Trigger → Wait → running state test) produced output with no errors.
